channel.title: Elarion (Playersafe)
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: House Yriveth
item.description: The Hollow Veil Holds The Eye has pressed upon this page. The page has not fully yielded. “The world must never forget us—nor truly find us.” A second gaze tried to close this line. The first veil refused.House Yriveth serves as ᛋᚺᛝ ᛏᛝᛃᚱᛇᚠ ᛃᛋᚺᛚ of Thalor Sylvae.The Eye attempted to name their roads, faces, and oaths. It found only and a door that remembered how to vanish. They remain diplomats, illusionists, and keepers of thresholds. More than this tries to surface, then folds away. Speak beyond the forest when the forest must not be seen. Carry messages that arrive as ᛠᛉᛟᛗᚹᛒᚠ ᛝᛗᚱᛉᛃᚲᛁ rather than ink. Preserve the boundary between invitation and intrusion. The Eye presses here. The script bends. The veil bends back. First attempt: Second attempt: ᚷᚢᛖᚷᚦᛏ ᛝᛁᛝᛇᛠᚦᚠ ᚢᛝᛏᚹᚦᛈ Third attempt: the page wrote itself smaller. Some letters remain alive. They shift because they are hiding, not because they are broken. A truth fully spoken is a truth placed in danger. Masks are not lies. They are mercy. Those who seek the hidden path must first be willing to lose the road. ᛉᚨ ᛝᛈᚱ ᛒᛁᛗ ᛒᚲᛟᚢᛒ ᚲᛏ ᚦᛇᛗ ᛈᛝᛗᛋᛋᛗ The Eye has seen the monolith attempt. House Yriveth has hidden the shape of what it saw.

item.title: 🌍 The World of Elarion
item.description:
A single-continent world with two distant islands, fractured kingdoms, magical secrets, and a growing threat from the north.Archive Notice This document is one of the few surviving continental summaries that has remained substantially intact. Individual records linked below may no longer agree with the information presented here. The Archive has elected to preserve this index rather than revise it. Type: Centralized monarchy Ruler: Queen Araleth VI Capital: Caer Thalor Magic: Strictly regulated; only licensed practitioners Military: Highly disciplined and loyal Nobility: Most noble houses are loyal to the queen Type: Alliance of free city-states Council Head: Lord Berrick of Myrhold Major Cities: Myrhold, Tarnath, Velmira, Cenwyn, Estrellan Magic: Lightly regulated, magic-users live freely Military: Independent militias and hired adventurers Type: Theocratic empire Ruler: Emperor Kavalh Capital: Varkhaz-Thul (surrounded by rivers of lava) Magic: Only divine and state-sanctioned arcane Known for: Master forges powered by volcanic flows Type: Druidic confederation Council: Council of Speakers Magic: Natural and spiritual magic, tied to leylines Known for: Deep forests, harmony with nature Obelion floats above Valewind, strengthening the leyline beneath it. Type: Independent holy city-state Governance: Council of Halos Religion: Center of the Church that worships Saint Vivi Known for: The Perfect Barrier that repels evil The Barrier protects the entire north. Saint Vivi is revered as a martyr who died casting the Barrier. Type: Elven forest kingdom Ruler: Queen Liraethe Velyndor Magic: Ancient, dream-bound, and song-based Culture: Reverent, quiet, rarely seen beyond the woods Type: Floating arcane citadel (Known as: The One True Tower) Governance: Archmage and Circle of Twelve Location: Mostly hovers above Valewind Known for: Research into barriers, leylines, and forgotten magic Obelion has discovered how to move the floating island, albeit slowly. HQ: Steelmark, an independent city not claimed by any kingdom Structure: Strict rank-based system: Porcelain, Copper, Bronze, Iron, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Mithril, Orichalcum, Adamantite Mithril and above require recommendation and legendary deeds Officially neutral and does not participate in wars. Former crescent-shaped island kingdom Now a ruined landmass overrun by monsters and rift-magic At its center stood the Eye of the World—once a trial site for a vessel meant to contain evil History In the previous campaign, the players failed to complete the trial at the Eye. The ancient king who once absorbed evil was corrupted, causing the island’s collapse and unleashing chaos. Location: Beyond the Titan’s Spine Mountains in the north Believed to be a result of Selune’s collapse and planar rift magic Now home to monsters, twisted magic, and aberrations ᚦᛟᛖ ᛞᚲᚹᚺᚹᛏ ᛗᛗᛃᛟᛇᚺᚦᛈ ᚦᛡᚹ Barrier ᛉᛏᚲᛈᛉ ᚢᚱᚲᚢ safe. ᚹᚢᚠᚲᛠ ᛞᛝᛚᛠᛚᛉᛠ ᛞᛁᛁᚠᛠᛚᚹᚹᚠᚷᛚᚺᛃ ᛡᚠᛁᚾᚲᛡ ᛚᚠᚢᛏᛞᛇᛁ ᛒᛇᛞᛡ ᛒᚹᛏᚠᚦᚲᛈ ᚱᚾᛋ ᚹᚱᛝᛡᚢᛉ ᚦᛈ ᛋᛇᛉ ᚲᛚᛇᛉ ᛏᚹ ᚠᛏᛃᛋᛏ ᛏᛚᚲᛟᛝᛗᛈᛈᚱᛝ ᚦᚹᛁᚠᛟᛈᚢᚷᛝᛈ ᛃᛟᛖᚨᛈᚦᚺᛈ ᛠᚺ ᛗᛠᛞᚱᚲᚷᛃᛝ ᛒᚠᛖ ᛝᚺᛖᚠᚢᚦ ᛝᚠᛞᛖᛝᛋᛈᛒᛒᛗ ᚾᛋᚷᚺ ᚾᛟᛁᛋᛁ ᚲᛖᛈ ᛠᛉᛈᛞᚦᚲᚷ ᚨᚨᚱᛠ ᛡᚷᛞ ᚷᛉᛇᚦᚨᚠ ᛗᛉᚹᛏᛉᛞᛖᛏᚢᛏᛋᛖ ᛡᛃᚺᚱᛒᛖ Magic is rare. Only the Gifted can level up or cast spells. Healing magic is incredibly uncommon outside of the Church, elves, and adventurers. Potions only work for the Gifted. Magic items (wands, weapons, tools, etc.) can be used by anyone, even those without the Gift. This makes enchanted tools and relics incredibly valuable, especially to nobles, military forces, and wealthy tradesfolk. Most magic tools are crafted with specific intent—many require training, activation rituals, or command words passed down through bloodlines or guilds. Powerful artifacts still require attunement by a Gifted soul. “The world isn’t breaking. It’s remembering.” — Unknown bard, one night before vanishing near the Shoreline In recent years, strange phenomena have begun to surface across the land—glimmering distortions, patches of withered earth, places where time moves oddly. Some call them rifts. Others whisper of cracks in the world.No one knows their true origin. Scholars speculate they may be remnants of ancient magic. Some blame the fall of Selune, the lost island that once held the Eye of the World. Others claim the world is simply unraveling—slowly, and with purpose. Rifts appear in places of old power: forgotten ruins, ancient battlefields, leyline nexuses, or cursed groves. They are often harmless to observe, though deeply unsettling: Colors shift, Sound warps, Gravity misbehaves. Magic acts unpredictably near them. Spells might flare, fizzle, or echo back twisted versions of themselves. Most rifts attract strange creatures. Others do nothing—for years. Tales persist of voices heard through rifts—familiar, dead, or never known. The Church: Cautious and divided. Some clergy call rifts divine trials; others fear corruption. The Adventurer’s Guild: Monitors and classifies rift zones. Bounties exist for rift investigation, recovery, or neutralization. The Magic Tower of Obelion: Publicly silent. Rumored to secretly observe, catalog, and experiment with rifts. Common Folk: Avoid rifts at all costs. Build shrines, wards, or stone circles nearby—just in case.
Cenwyn A rural city where sudden auroras and eerie rainfall are becoming common. Locals report strange dreams, missing livestock, and lights in the woods.Obsidian Shoreline An abandoned port town where a rift shimmers offshore. Fishermen claim something watches from the sea floor.The Withered Hollow A once-lush grove now completely dead. No birds sing, and tools rust overnight when left in the clearing. Passing through a rift may change a person—body or soul. Artifacts found near rifts sometimes exhibit sentience or warped magic. Some bards sing of a time before the rifts, when the world was whole. And how that world might ᛒᛏᛒᛖᚢᛋ ᚢᚹ ᚷᚲᛃᚠᛚᚾᛁᛝᚠ ᛖᛚ ᚱᚠᛚ ᚷᛋᚷᚺᚱ ᚨᛒᛈᛖᛡ

item.title: Eye of the World
item.description: Archive FailureThis page has exceeded the archive's restoration threshold.Further reconstruction attempts have been permanently suspended.No authenticated description remains.Location: Purpose: Origin: Current State: The archive confirms that this article once contained several independent historical accounts.Every surviving copy is now identical.None contain readable text.The following records have suffered progressive degradation after linking to this article. Selune Shattered Sight Rift Phenomena Ancient History Further correlation is under investigation.Repeated attempts to restore this document have resulted in additional information loss elsewhere in the archive.Restoration has been abandoned.The archive no longer searches for this page.It waits for it to return.

item.title: Shattered Sight
item.description: Archive FailureThis document has been rewritten repeatedly.Each revision contains less information than the previous one.Automatic restoration has been disabled.There was once a complete article here.The archive confirms this.No copy survives.The original account cannot be reconstructed.Every surviving manuscript terminates at the same point.No explanation accompanies the loss.Primary Doctrine: Foundational Text: Known Rituals: Object of Worship: Attempts to restore these records have failed.The existence of independent cells has been verified.All identifying information has disappeared.Recovered descriptions conflict.Recovered sketches fade from stored copies within days.No authenticated symbol remains.Several historical incidents have been attributed to this organization.The supporting evidence no longer exists.Whether the incidents occurred at all cannot now be determined.Archivists are advised to discontinue further reconstruction.Repeated attempts correlate with progressive loss of unrelated records.The phenomenon appears contagious.Some knowledge does not wish to be remembered.Some knowledge remembers you.

item.title: Adventurer’s Guild
item.description: Guild Bulletin 14-77The Guild Archive remains largely unaffected.However, numerous external references now point to incomplete, contradictory, or missing records.Guild historians have been instructed to preserve physical copies whenever possible.The Adventurer’s Guild is a transnational organization that offers contracts, certifications, and logistical support to those rare individuals gifted with the ability to level up. The Guild maintains neutrality in all conflicts and does not serve any crown or kingdom.Its main headquarters is not part of any nation—it exists as an independent town, fortified and self-sustaining, where adventurers train, rest, and report. Only those bearing a recognized Guild emblem may enter the central tower. Type: Independent Free Town Leadership: The Grandmaster of Quests and the Circle of Guildmasters Alignment: True Neutral Motto: "For coin, cause, or challenge—walk the path." The Guild operates in tiers, both for adventurer ranking and organizational authority. Every regional guild reports to the central HQ. Porcelain Copper Bronze Iron Silver Gold Platinum Mithril Orichalcum Adamantite Note: Mithril and higher ranks can only be achieved through extraordinary deeds and a formal recommendation by a Guildmaster or higher. The HQ town—known as Steelmark—is built on neutral land between major kingdoms.It includes: Training grounds Contract halls Arcane registry Alchemical shops Small arena for mock trials and public duels Airship pad (restricted access) The Guild does not take sides in wars. Adventurers acting in war are considered mercenaries, not Guild agents. Guild contracts are public record. Failure to report completed contracts may result in suspension or expulsion. Grandmaster Reign Evershade Mistress Vallan of Maps Eldren Flintmark Lyss of the 12th Bell Korrak the Bold Virelia Dravandor Confederacy Myriath Obelion Arken Dominion (outdated) Valewind Pactlands Thalor Sylvae Archivist's NoteRecent expeditions have returned claiming that several nations possess archives which no longer match Guild records.At present, the Guild has found no evidence that its own ledgers have been altered.The discrepancy remains under investigation.

item.title: Obelion
item.description: Archive Integrity ReportThe following document is maintained by the Circle of Five.Unauthorized alterations have been detected.Automatic restoration remains active.Obelion—known as the One True Tower—is the last unified mage tower in the world.This paragraph has been restored 17 times.Current copy verified.The floating island remains above the Valewind Pactlands.No measurable alteration has occurred.Attempts to remove geographic references continue to fail.Observation 41External force attempted to remove references to regional leyline amplification.The original wording could not be recovered.Current findings continue to support the same conclusion.The Tower strengthens the surrounding leyline.Cause remains under investigation.Current priorities remain unchanged. Replicating the Perfect Barrier. Studying planar instability. Investigating rift phenomena. Accepting gifted mages regardless of origin. Neutrality remains official policy.Diplomatic records remain intact.No successful alterations detected.The Circle of Five remains unchanged. Archmage Veridan High Seer Elora Vell Master Ghallor Tane Scribe-Lord Kael of Chains Mistress Aivenna of Glass [Success] Restored 12 missing paragraphs.[Failure] Original wording unrecoverable.[Success] Cross-reference verified through three independent memory anchors.[Failure] Unknown entity continues altering historical records.[Success] Alteration pattern now under active study.The archive now studies the thing that studies the archive.

item.title: Virelia
item.description: ROYAL ARCHIVESThis document has been recopied repeatedly.Each revision contains less than the last.Virelia remains.Its official history does not.The Ministry of Records confirms this section existed.The Ministry of Records cannot produce a surviving copy.The following houses continue to serve the Crown.Their records remain under active restoration. House Elstrae House Vaeron House Lorentis House Branmar House Thorne House Delyssar Capital: Caer ThalorRoyal Census: Military Registry: Civil Archive: Official records continue to list the Royal Legion.The number of soldiers differs in every surviving copy.No officer has been able to explain why.Every diplomatic ledger produced after the recent revisions has contained missing pages.Replacement copies lose the same pages within days.The Royal Archives have suspended further restoration attempts.Archive Revision 117 concluded that the losses are not the result of fire, decay, theft, or clerical error.No further explanation has been entered into the record.Order preserves kingdoms.It cannot preserve forgotten words.

item.title: Valewind Pactlands
item.description: Living ArchiveSome knowledge was defended.Some knowledge was forgotten.The forest does not distinguish between the two.The Pactlands endure.The names of those who ruled them no longer matter.The trees have outlived every record.This section was intentionally left unfinished.No evidence suggests it was ever completed. The leylines still flow.No surviving document agrees where.No druid questioned this.The following names remain rooted. Greenthorn Canopy Stonegrove Veins Mossmere Basin Sablefen Their descriptions have returned to the soil.Relationships change.The forest expects them to.No permanent record remains.The Pactlands have never relied upon walls.Nor upon words.Only the living remember how they are defended.The oldest trees surrender every leaf.None mistake this for defeat.

item.title: Thalor Sylvae
item.description: Dream ArchiveThis record no longer remains in the same form between viewings.Multiple revisions exist simultaneously.Thalor Sylvae is remembered.Its borders are not.The forest appears unchanged across every surviving map.The paths through it do not.The oldest copies describe harmony.Newer copies omit entire centuries.One version claims no cities were ever built.Another insists the trees remember them all.The Queen continues to dream.No record disputes this.Everything written after that begins to diverge.Some accounts describe a sleeping monarch.Others describe someone who has already awakened.A few insist she has always been speaking.No scholar has successfully determined which version was written first.Only the names remain fixed. House Velasira House Ennarion House Saelthir House Yriveth House Elenrath Their duties seem to rearrange themselves whenever the archive is copied.Location:Population:Current State:Present.The forest still refuses unwanted visitors.How it accomplishes this has become uncertain.Every surviving description differs.None contradict the outcome.Diplomatic records have become dreamlike.Only one statement remains unchanged. The forest still watches. The final paragraph has resisted every attempt at revision.The Eye may observe the dream.It cannot decide which dream is true.

item.title: Myriath
item.description: Sanctified ArchiveThis record has been subjected to repeated attempts at alteration.Divine wards continue to preserve portions of the original text.Myriath is a shining city of marble domes, golden spires, and holy silence.The remainder of the original description cannot be verified.The Barrier still stands.Attempts to alter this record have repeatedly failed.The exact workings remain protected.No surviving copy differs.The Council of High Clerics still governs.The Sanctum of Radiant Peace remains.Regional Priories continue to answer to Myriath.One line has resisted every attempt at revision: The city has not fallen. Much of this section has been damaged.Only fragments remain. Do no evil. Serve the Light. Defend the innocent. The remaining text has become unreadable.Standing Army: Inner Sanctum: The final paragraph refuses alteration. Evil still cannot cross the Barrier. Most diplomatic records have degraded.Only the names remain. Virelia Dravandor Confederacy Obelion Thalor Sylvae Valewind Pactlands Information regarding this relic has been partially sealed.Attempts to reconstruct its purpose produce conflicting copies.One sentence survives intact. It has not yet gone dark. Every attempt to remove this section from the archive has failed.The record remains under divine protection.No further information is available.Even the Eye does not look upon every light unchallenged.

item.title: Dravandor Confederacy
item.description: Archive Integrity CompromisedThe existence of the Confederacy is confirmed.Everything beyond that is disputed.The Dravandor Confederacy was once described here in detail.No surviving copy agrees with another.Scholars cannot determine whether the discrepancies arose from war......or from something that wished the records to disagree.The names remain.The descriptions do not. Myrhold — Tarnath — Velmira — Cenwyn — Estrellan — Leadership: Capital: Authority: Records conflict regarding every succession.References to magical regulation remain inconsistent.Several copies mention the Adventurer's Guild.Others insist no such agreement ever existed.Mentions of Obelion appear throughout the surviving records, though never in the same context twice.Standing Army: Regional Forces: Current Campaigns: Every diplomatic record has been altered.Only one conclusion appears consistently across every surviving copy.Someone wanted history remembered...just not correctly.The Eye does not erase nations.It erases certainty.

item.title: Arken Dominion
item.description: National Status Destroyed The Arken Dominion no longer exists as a sovereign nation. The Arken Dominion was once a theocratic empire fueled by divine zealotry, volcanic industry, and the legacy of the dragon hunts.It ceased to function as a nation following the Cataclysm at Varkhaz-Thul, when a single mage unleashed a ninth-level spell of catastrophic scale upon the Dominion’s heartland.Witnesses reported the appearance of a second star above the Dominion shortly before the destruction began.Moments later, Varkhaz-Thul and much of the surrounding territory were consumed by an arcane impact that shattered the capital, broke the central government, and scattered the Dominion Legions.The exact spell used has not been confirmed. Surviving scholars classify the event only as:Capital: Destroyed Government: Dissolved Emperor Kavalh: Presumed dead Dominion Legions: Scattered Known Survivors: Unknown National Status: Fallen This page previously contained information describing the Arken Dominion prior to its destruction.Many records were lost during the Cataclysm. Surviving accounts remain incomplete, contradictory, or politically compromised. “The sky fell upward before it fell down.” — Last recovered testimony from Varkhaz-Thul Last Updated: Following the Fall of the Arken Dominion

item.title: House Vaeron
item.description: “All debts are paid in the end.” This record was once complete.Names remain. Sums do not.The house is acknowledged. Its worth is not. Assets: Debts: Oaths: Blood-price: Every total returns empty. The signatures survived. The terms did not. The witnesses deny reading them. The Eye has balanced the account.

item.title: House Thorne
item.description: Privileged Record — Withheld by Order of the Crown This entry remains in the archive only to confirm that a noble house by this name exists. Recovered content has been narrowed, obscured, or removed following unauthorized monolith inquiry. “The rose is lovely… but mind the thorn.” The remainder of the house motto has been struck from approved circulation. House Thorne serves Virelia in matters of , , and . Their work is considered necessary, loyal, and unsuitable for public explanation. Earlier copies of this page contained operational details. Those copies have been collected. Name: Title: Public Appearance: Known Authority: Any surviving portraits have been removed from common view. Location: Substructure: Prisoner records: The map no longer marks the estate. Roads that once led there now end in ordinary mist. Truth is a tool. Loyalty is a blade. The enemy within must never be allowed to know it has been seen. The Eye has seen the inquiry. House Thorne has seen the reader. Witness A: Recanted. Witness B: Relocated. Witness C: Never existed. Witness D:

item.title: House Lorentis
item.description: Blighted Record “From soil to sword, we nourish the realm.” The ink beneath this motto has browned like dead wheat. The fields no longer answer. Once beloved. The stands among miles of . Pilgrims once gathered here. The harvests were plentiful. honestyhard workbalance Respected by . Trusted by . Watched by . Forgotten. Forgotten. Year 1 — plentiful Year 2 — plentiful Year 3 — plentiful Year 4 — plentiful Year 5 — plentiful Year 6 — plentiful Year 7 — plentiful Year 8 — plentiful Year 9 — plentiful Year 10 — Year 11 — Year 12 — Year 13 — Something has taken root.

item.title: House Elstrae
item.description: “We are the blade the Queen may always draw.” By Order of the Crownshield This house record has been placed beneath martial seal. Cause of closure: unauthorized recovery and interpretation of monolithic script. Witnessing authority: Reader clearance: denied. House Name: Seat: Current Head: Military Relevance: Any surviving description has been withdrawn from public circulation. Overview — sealed. Current Head — sealed. Seat — sealed. Values & Training — sealed. Reputation Among Other Houses — sealed. The Eye has seen the attempt. The record will not arm you further.

item.title: House Delyssar
item.description: The Eye Has Seen the Attempt MONOLITH CORRELATION RECOGNIZED FURTHER READING REFUSED This record no longer opens for those who have followed the monolith too far. The page is present. The words are not permitted to arrive. You were observed before you understood what observation meant. Previous entry body — withheld. Linked references — withheld. Recovered annotations — withheld. Do not return to the stone seeking a second answer.

item.title: House Branmar
item.description: What was written here has been fired beyond reading The page still smells faintly of coal, rain, and hot iron. No flame is visible. “In fire, something answered.” “The hammer fell three times. The fourth strike was not ours.” All names, holdings, craft records, and alliances have burned cleanly out of the document.The ink did not blacken. It retreated from the page as if the words had been too close to a furnace.Pressed into the lower margin is a shallow mark, like a seal made by a heated thumb. Do not reforge what has chosen to cool.

item.title: Stonegrove Veins
item.description: “The stone remembers.” The record is present, but no longer ordered The stone remembers. The rain had not stopped for seven days. Someone was singing below the roots. The crystal cracked when she spoke his true name. I do not remember my son's face. We should never have climbed the tower. Three voices argued in the dark. The fourth remained silent. The vote was unanimous. We buried them anyway. Memory is sacred. A child pressed both palms to the cavern wall and heard her grandmother answer. The dragon's shadow passed over red stone. No one spoke after the hymn ended. The mountain learned our names before we did. I remember the sound of a gate closing. I remember hands covered in ash. I remember a crown too heavy for a living head. I remember the sea pulling away from the shore. I remember a name being cut from every song. I remember the Eye opening. Names remain impressed in the stone, but no tongue agrees on how they were spoken. Someone stood here once. The stone remembers their weight. The stone does not remember their face. The true record is buried beneath memories that are not yours.

item.title: Sablefen
item.description: A record once kept here. A promise now keeps it elsewhere. The entry has not been deleted. It was bargained away. old terms remain binding Sablefen is acknowledged as a place within the Valewind Pactlands.No further public description is currently reliable. Names, customs, rites, inhabitants, and known tensions return as damp marks when copied. Do not ask the fen for what it has already promised to hide. the first name   the second oath   the thing beneath the waterThe ink grows darker when read aloud. I wrote the full account once. By morning, the page was blank and my left hand smelled of marshwater.

item.title: Mossmere Basin
item.description: The ink has run. Mossmere Basin Most of this entry has bled through the page. The surviving paper is cold to the touch. “The waters remember...”“...Speaker Mira Hollowroot...”“...during the Flood Moon...”“Do not drink from ——————”“...the basin answered in someone else’s voice.” Several attempts have been made to restore this record. Each recovered copy degrades within hours. The missing text does not appear burned, scraped, cursed, or hidden. It appears to have flowed away. I copied this three times. Each copy lost different words. I no longer remember which version was original.

item.title: Greenthorn Canopy
item.description: “We do not rule the tree. We ask. And sometimes, it refuses.” The living archive has closed around this page The bark remembers the shape of the words, but not the words themselves. The public record confirms only that Greenthorn Canopy exists within the Valewind Pactlands. Further description has been swallowed by root and shade. Name: Greenthorn Canopy Region: withheld Speaker: withheld Customs: withheld Recent disturbances: withheld Several copied versions of this page have produced only pressed leaves between blank paragraphs. The forest has learned caution.

item.title: Velmira
item.description: A city remembered only in safe shapes. The fuller account once held here has been covered. The page still names the city, but refuses its deeper face. A Mask Has Been Laid Over This Record Velmira remains listed as a city of the Dravandor Confederacy.Everything beyond that has been made unreliable. Districts, rulers, customs, and internal politics — obscured. Arcane practices and local magical habits — obscured. Recent disappearances, storms, masks, and rumors — obscured. The censored sections do not appear burned, deleted, or edited. They appear as though the words were never willing to stand still. Behind every mask is another mask. Behind the last one, nothing looks back. Recovered text refuses alignment

item.title: Tarnath
item.description: A name remains. The rest has been made difficult to remember. A hand has passed over this page The old entry has not been deleted. It has been refused. Name: Tarnath Region: Known for: Current concerns: Several copied versions of this article disagree with one another. The safest version is the emptiest. Do not force the page open. Some things close because something on the other side is holding them shut.

item.title: Myrhold
item.description: The page refuses the witness This place is acknowledged. Its shape is not offered. The public record once contained a summary here.Names, customs, defenses, leaders, and tensions have been removed. The ink did not blacken. It simply forgot how to be read. removed removed removedremoved removedremoved removed removed Copies made before the alteration now open to blank margins. To know a thing is to give it a doorway.

item.title: Estrellan
item.description: The page remembers less than it should What was written here has turned its face away. This entry is acknowledged as existing. No further reliable record remains available.Attempts to restore prior text return blank copies, corrupted headings, or pages whose ink has faded before being read. Do not name what has chosen not to be seen.

item.title: Cenwyn
item.description: Entry Restricted This record is no longer available for public viewing. Archive Status: SANITIZED // External Recall Denied // Revision Chain Broken The requested article has been removed from the player-facing archive. Location: Known For: Local History: Associated Threats: No approved summary remains. Text fragments could not be restored. Original page text — removed. Cached article body — removed. External references — removed. Searchable keywords — removed. This page now contains only the minimum record necessary to confirm that a page once existed here. Recall Warning Attempting to reconstruct this entry from memory is discouraged. Further inquiries may result in additional record loss.

item.title: Index
item.description: “To understand the world, begin at its name.” A singular record, whispered through leyline, lore, and time. This vault holds the threads of a realm shaped by rift and relic, by oath and ruin.→ 🌍 The World of Elarion “Before the stars kept watch, before the mountains knew their weight, the world was sealed not by stone nor flame, but by a name no tongue remembers. Speak it not aloud. It listens still.”

item.title: House Elenrath
item.description: ᚾᛠᛖᚾᚲ ᛉᛁᛡ ᛉᛒᛡᛉᛟᛡᛁ ᛋᚱ ᚹᛗᚹᛖᚨᚢ ᚾᛝᛞᛖᛇ ᛡᚱᚾᛡᛟᛒᛟᛞ ᚨᛡᛁ ᚠᚦᛈᚺᚲᛇᚠᛁ ᛃᛖ ᛠᛒᚱ ᚹᚷᛁᛏᛞᚹ ᚾᛠᚺᛏᛁᛉᛇᚷᛒ ᛠ ᛋᛟᚺᚦᛚᚷᚲᛠᛖᛟᚨᛚ ᛠᛠᛈᛖᛉᛋᛗᛉᚹᛃᛉᚾᛝᛒ ᚨᛉᛝ ᛚᚢᚠᛏᛇᛉᚲᚢᛟᚦ ᛞᛈ ᛃᚠᛇ ᚱᛃᚱᚢᛚᛚ ᚷᛏᚾᚢᚲᚨᛖ ᛠᛁᚲᛖ ᛗᛖ ᛝᛇᛈ ᚹᚨᚠᛞᛃᚾᛉ ᚨᚠᛗᚾᚾᛉᛞ ᚨᛞᛞᛟ ᛈᚺᛁᛡᛁᚹᛃ ᛝᛉᚲᛝ ᚨᛞᚲ ᛗᚠᛋᛇᛇ ᚷᚺᚦᛗᛖ ᛝᚠᚠᚱᚨ ᛠᛈᛁᛖᛋᚾ ᛃᛖᛝ ᛉᛒᛋᛞᚱᚷ ᚱᛞᚨᚢᛏᚺ ᚨᛁᛠᛒᛇᛁ ᚨᚱᛉ ᛖᛈᚲᚱᛡᛖᚹᚨᛚᚹ ᛒᛖᛁᚠᛚᚺᛠ ᚾᛈᛝ ᛝᚾᛏᚨᚺᛇ ᛡᚲᚷᚨᛒᚦ ᛃᛚ ᛇ ᛟᛖᛗᚠᚾᛇ ᚲᚱᛖᛋᚾᛟᛖᚱᚨ ᛗᛖ ᛈᛃᛚᚠᚱ ᛗᚨᛡᚦᛚᛇᚱᛚ ᚺᛃᚠᛗᛗᚠᛠᚾᛠᚦ ᛉᚺᛝᚷᛚᛋᚾᛋ ᛠᛠᛒᚹᛇᚢᛠ ᚾᚢᛚ ᚨᛁᚲᛗ ᛇᚢᛚᛞᛖ ᛇᚲᛏᛗᚲ ᛁᚷᚾᛟᚺ ᚨᛠ ᛠᚾᚷᚾᛇᚹ ᛏᛡᛞᛉᛟᛒᛃᛈᛡᛈᚦ ᛠᛗᛞᛝᚠ ᚱᚢᛝ ᚷᛞᛞᛞ ᚱᛃ ᛠᚱᛝᚲᛈᛗ ᛈᛖᛞᛞᚷᛋᚲᛈᚨ ᛞᛞᛞᚲᛉᛋᛉᚹᛠᛚᛟᛖ ᛏᚹᛚ ᛗᚷᚹᛁᛈᛡ ᚨᚱᛡᚠᛈᚠ ᛚ ᛖᚹᚲᛗᛃᚦᚷᚾᚠ ᛁᚠᚦᛈᛉᚲᛚᛈᛖ ᛞᛗᛗᛡᛇᚢᛇ ᛞᚦᚨ ᛡᛈᚦᛁᚹᛒ ᛗᛚᚹᛈᛚᛗᛁᚨ ᚹᛋᛡᛡᚲᚠᛏᛈᛃᛃ ᛈᛈ ᚱᚢᛁᛟᛡᛈᛏᛝᛋ ᛞ ᛠᛃᛈᛁᚠ ᛗᚲᛒᛒᚨ ᚢᛃᛒᛁᛁᛈ ᛡᛈᚷᚠᛠᚷᛁᚱᚦᛋᛋ ᚨᛏ ᛗᛝᛗ ᛖᛉᛈ ᚱᛉᚾᛚᛝ ᚦ ᚹᚦᛇᛉ ᚺᚹᚲᛈ ᛖᛡᛒ ᚾᛡᚠ ᛁᛝᚲᛝᛚᚲ ᛇᚨᚢᚹᛖᚾᚲᛟ ᚾ ᚠᚾᛟᛗᚢ ᚾᛈᚨᛁᛇᚢ ᚨᛈᛋ ᚷᛚᚺᛉᛒᛡᚦ ᛃᚹᛠᚦᚱᚱᛡᛗ ᚹᛈᛏᛖ ᛖᚢᚦᚢᛗ ᛉᛡᛃ ᛈᛞᛠᛒᛒᛁ ᛋᛗᛚᛁᛃᛖ ᚠᚦᛚᚢ ᛉᛏᚠᛗᛚ ᛠᛇᛏ ᚠᚲᚱᛝᚹ ᛠ ᛡᚢᛏ ᚢᚦᚺᛋᚦᛇᛇᛖᛉ ᚺᛏᛇᛚᚨᚹᚦ ᛠᛖᚾ ᚢᚷᛁᛁᛟᛚᛈᚨ ᚠᛉᛟᛟᛇᚷᛡᛇᛟᛏ ᛋᚺᛟᚦᛈ ᚱᛝᛖ ᛇᛏᛈᛞᚢᚦ ᛡᚲ ᚺᚷᛏᛟᛇ ᛉᛠᛚᚺ ᛗᛡᚲᛏᛠᛁᛏᛒᚾ ᚦᛏᛁᛉᛃᛝᛞᛖ

item.title: House Ennarion
item.description: ᚠᛉᚨᛏ ᚠᚹᛃᚷᚲ ᛡᛃᚹᛚ ᛟᛟᛇᛉᛟᛒᚾ ᛞᚠᛚ ᛏᛝᛝᚨᚢᛒ ᛒᛚᚱᛡ ᛡᛏᛈᚢᚲ ᚢᛞᛏᛞᛈ ᚦᚠᚠᛃᛚᛚᛗᚦ ᚠᚠᚹᛃᛉᛒᛁᚲ ᚾᛒᛒ ᚺᚠᚠᚢᛖᚾᚦ ᛈᛟᛠᚢᚱ ᛇᛏ ᚷᚹᚨᛒᚠᛃ ᛋᚹᛈᚢᚠᚢᚾᛉᚲ ᛖᚷᚾᛁᚱᚦᛃᛋᛒ ᚦᚷᚹᛇᛠᚨᛈᛖᛁ ᛡᛉᛁ ᛡᚲᛞᛝᛈᚨᛇᛁ ᚾᚨᛞᛗᛝᛚᛠ ᚱᚷᚹᚨ ᛒᛃᚷᚷᛖᚨ ᚨᚹᛗ ᛟᛝᛝᚨᛈᛇᛏ ᛁᛟᚨᚦ ᛞᚢᛟ ᛗᛝᛏᛁᛚ ᛈᚾᛁᛁᛃᛗᚲᛇ ᛟᚢᛡᚾᛈᛇᛈᛡ ᛠᛠᛖ ᛟᚺᛞᛇᚢᛚᚨᚢᚱᛃᛗᛞ ᛞᚠᛗᛃᚠᚨᚷᛁᛇ ᛞᛡᚨᛠᚢ ᛇᛟᛚ ᛒᛋᛏᚠ ᛈᚺᛒᛗᚺ ᚨᚨᚨᛝᚨᛃᚹᚱᛈ ᛈᚾᚢᛖᛝᛝᛞᛇᚲᛃᛒᚱᛋᚺᚨ ᚦᚹᚢᛡ ᛏᛝᛟᛁ ᛡᛖᛞ ᚠᚹᚦᛁᚨᛝ ᚹᛗᚲᛒ ᛃᛟᚷᛒ ᚱ ᛏᛃᛃᚺ ᛗᛟᚨᛡᚷᚾᚠ ᛟᛇᛉ ᚺᚲᛁᚢᛝᚺᛠᛁᚾ ᛝᛡᛁᚺᛞ ᛝᛝᛒᚹ ᚹᛝᚠᛖᛠᛒᛟ ᛏᚹᚨᛞ ᛡᛋ ᛇᛡᛡᚦᚨ ᛠᚹᚦᚷ ᛁᛞᚾᛗᚨ ᚾᛟᚢᛟ ᛉᛏᚢᛟᚠᛖᚱᛞᛃᛒᛚᛉᛖᛟᛟᛃ ᚦᛇᚹᛏᚹᚠ ᛈᛇᚾ ᛝᛈᛖᚹᚷ ᛗᛟᚨ ᛗᛒᛒᛋᚲᛏᚠᛚ ᛞᛖᛞᚺ ᛃ ᛖᛟᚢᛃᛟᚨ ᚾᚱᛏᛡᚲᚦᛠ ᛈᚾᛉᛉᛠᛏᚲᛝᛈ ᚢᛠᚲᚠ ᛒᛠᛉᛡᛞᛝᚹᚱᛇᛚᛞ ᛇ ᚹᛚᚹᛁᛃᚠᛟᚦᛈ ᚷᚾᚹᛡᛉᚨᛉ ᛞᚦᛉᛁᛞᛈᛈᚦᛟ ᛚᛁᚨᛗᚦᛉᛟᛟᛉᚾ ᛡ ᛡᚷᛠᛃᛏᚷᚹᚹ ᚹᛈᚨᛃᛃᛇᛃᛖ ᛃᚠᛒᚦᛉᛏᚲ ᛞᚠᛗᚷ ᛗᚱᚠᛁᛗᛇᛞ ᛃᛡᚢ ᛈᛃᚦᛟᛉᚠ ᛃᚱᛏᚦ ᛋᛇᛉᚷᛇᛒᛝᛇᛏᛒ ᛗᛃ ᛚᛋᛃᛉᛟᚷᚺᛋ ᛋᚱᚾᛠᛃᛚᛃᛏ ᛃᛉᛗᛋᛚᛟᚱ ᛇᛏᛠ ᛁᚱᛟᚷᚱᛒᛃᚲᛝᛋᛟᚢᚲᚾᛇᛃ ᛚ ᛃᚲᚺᛡᛋᛝ ᚺᛇᛉᛝᚲ ᚾᚺᚺᚱᛇᛉ ᛈᛗᛏ ᚠᛠᚱᚢᛃ ᛒᛚ ᛡᛈᚹᚠᛠ ᛗᚾᛞᚲᚷ ᚷ ᛋᛈᛡᛈᛡ ᛠᛠᛖ ᛒᚷᛝ ᚹᛁ ᚲᚺᚦᛗᚠᛖᛃ ᚾᚦᚷ ᚠᚨᚾᚱᚺᛝ ᛃᚷᛗᛚᚹᛒᛈᛉᛃ ᚠᛖᚹᚠᛈᛇ ᛈᛋᛠᚹᛉᛗᚲᚺ ᛉᚱ ᛏᛝᛃᚹ ᛋᚲᚨᛃᚷᚺᚠᛈᛏ ᚲ ᚨᛞᚲᛞ ᛚᛞᛈᛋᛠᛠᛝ ᚲᛚᛇ ᛒᛗᛋᛁᚾᛞ ᚨᚠᛠᚾ ᚦᛚ ᚨᛖᚷᚺᚠᛁᛒᛃᚺ ᛁᛡ ᚷᛃᚲᛞᚱᚲᛚᛃᛡ ᚱ ᚱᛁᛝ ᛉᛃᛈᚾ ᚱᚦᚠᛞᛋᚱᛖ

item.title: House Velasira
item.description: ᛋᚲᚹ ᛃᛏ ᛏᚷᛖ ᛏᚨᛉᚲᛒ ᚹ ᚷᛖᛉᛞᚷᚺ ᚺᛋ ᚾᚺᚺᛚᚠ ᛞᚺᚢ ᚲᛈᛡᚲᚷᛟᚱᚨ ᚨᚺᚹᛁᚾ ᚱᚨᚨᛉᛏᛝᛁᚢ ᚷᛒ ᚾ ᚾᛇᚦᛃᛗᚠ ᛚᛋᛝᛁᛞ ᛇᚠ ᚢᛡᛃᛚᛚ ᚢᚢᚠᛟᛈᛞᛉ ᛒᚺᚲᛖ ᛈᚱᛠᛝᛒᛝᚺᛃᛗᛈ ᛉᚲᛃ ᛁᛚᚷᛃᛒᛋᚱ ᛉᚹ ᛟᛚᚦᚹᛠ ᛠᛏᛝᛝᚦᚷᛞᚢ ᚢᛠᛗᛋᛉᚷᛚᛋ ᛒᛚᚱ ᚨᛏᛗᚦᛚᚦᚾ ᛒᛡᛡ ᚢᛟᛉᛞᚾ ᛁᛖᛝᛇᚱᚠᛡ ᛉᚹ ᛈᛉᚺ ᛉᛖᚹᛚᛚᚦᛈᚢᛇᚱᚱ ᛟᚠᛗᚲᛗᚷᚺᚨᛉᛠ ᚠᚹᛗᛏᛃ ᚱᛚᚹᚦᛈᚷᛈᛃᚱ ᚾᛚ ᛁᛞᛁᚦᛒᛝ ᛒᚱᛁᚨᛟᚹᛉᚾᛠᚠᛁ ᚺᛇᛋ ᛁᚾᛏᚷᛝ ᛇᚠᚷᛃᛞᚢᛚ ᛈᛒᛞᛞ ᚹᛇᚱᛋ ᛃᛞᚺ ᚨᚾᚢᚾᚢᚦᛡ ᚢᛏᛒᚲᛚᚷ ᚺᛏᚱᚲ ᚾᛞᚦᚺ ᛝᛖᛋ ᛁᚾᛠᚨᚦᚱ ᚹᛃᚱᛒᛟᚹᚨᛒᛠᛖᚢ ᛉᚢᛈᚹᛠᛠᛠ ᚹᛚᛒ ᛋᚷᚺᛠᛟ ᛏᛖᛁᚷᛋᛒᚲ ᚺᛃᛃᚢ ᚱᚨᛖᚠᛈ ᚨᛗ ᛟᚱᚹᛞᛟᛋᚦᛝᛈ ᛟᚹᛃᛡ ᚾᛚᛞ ᚨᛖᚨ ᚹᛞᛉᛈᛗᛁᚢ ᛗᛖ ᚺᚦᚠᛏ ᚺᛁᚢᛚᚷᛉᛠᚢᛏ ᛃᛟᚷᛖᚱᛁᛈᛏᚢᛒ ᛇᛉᚷ ᛋᚢᛞᛡᛡᚷ ᛋᚺᛈᛉᚹᛒᚺᚦ ᛋᛏᛏᛡᛈᛗᛒᛉᚷ ᛖᛖ ᛒᛃᛃ ᛚᚾᛠᛃᛏᛃᚹᚱᛇᛞᛇ ᚾᛒᛉᚾᚷᚾᛏᚹᚾᚦ ᛈᚱᚾᚢᛁ ᚨᛝᛃ ᛟᚲᛏᛋᚢ ᚾᛇᚾᛝᚢᛞ ᛋ ᚢᛡᛟᛈᛉᛏᛝ ᚾᚾ ᛈᚹᚹ ᚱᛏᛝᚾᛒ ᛏᚺ ᚷᚺᚺᛒᛠᛝ ᛟ ᛁᚠᛈ ᛠᛗᚺᛁᚨᛝ ᛋᛉᛚᚨᛚᛁᚱᛏᛠᛠᛡᚦ ᚦᚲᚦᚷᛋᛡ ᚺᛒᚾ ᛖᛒᚾᛒᚺᚦᛟ ᛖᚲᚹᚨᚱᛞᛠᛃ ᚱᚷᚦᚢᛉ ᛁ ᚢᛋᛗᚾᚷᚾᚱᛖ ᛟᛝᛋᛖᚾᛝ ᛋᚦᚺᛗᛞᛗᛒ ᛚᚠᚦ ᚲᛏᚾᚱᛋᛚ ᚾᚲ ᚾᛋᚱ ᛡᚢᛠᚾᚠᛚᚾ ᚺᚨᛏᛡᚾᛠᚦ ᚠᚺ ᚢᚾᛃ ᛚᛗᛁᛝᛉᛇ ᛏᚨᚦ ᚨᛖᛗᛏᚢᛒᛗᚢᛉᛒ ᛃᛡᚱᛞᛠᛃᛝᛡᛚ ᚢᛁᛃᚢᛗᛚᛈ ᛡᛖᚱ ᚢᚢᚨᛡᚢᚹᛁᚷᚷᛉᛋᛏᚢᚾᚹᛟ ᚹᛇᛡᚲᚲᛝᛏᚲ ᛒᚹᚱᛚᛋ ᛃᛝᚾᛖᛋᛇ ᛝᚦᚢᛟᛚᛠ ᛞᚲᛡᚱ ᛏᛇᚺ ᛚᛃᛠᚹᛡᚠᚢ ᚦᛚᛚᚺᚷ ᚦᛏᛇᛖᛝ ᚱᚾᚦᛖ ᚹᛚᚾᛝᛞᚷᚱᛖᚷᛒᚹᚱᛇᚾᚷ ᚱᚺᚢᛏᛡᛉᚠᚠ ᛚᛉᛞᚠᛠ ᚲᚦᛟᚷ ᚱᛃᛈᚨ ᚢᚨᚨᚦᛈᛚᛁᛠᛃ ᛠᛉᚦ ᚠᚲᚾᚺᚢᚨᛉᚹ ᚢᚦᛏᚠ ᚺ ᚾᛠᛡᛖᚷᛏᛖᚠ ᛚᛠᛗᚷᛈᛠᛁ ᚦᛈᛝᛝᚹ ᛡᚺᚢᚾᛚ ᛒᚷ ᚢᛡᛟᛁᚨᛡ ᛉᛖ ᛇᚹᛡᛋᛠᛖᛚ ᛠᛞᛞ ᚦᛒᛠᛋ ᚲᛇᛇ ᛏᚹᚷ ᚠᛚᛖᛏᛏᛁ ᛟᚦᛝᛈ ᛇᛋᚱᛞ ᛝᚾᛝ ᛞᚠᛟᛃᛞ ᛏᛈᛃᚦᛋ

item.title: House Saelthir
item.description: ᛗᛡᛈᛖ ᚺᚲᛋᛖᛋᚦᚦ ᛡᛋᛡᛗᛒᛟ ᛝᚠᛇᛒᛠᛁ ᛖᛠ ᛖᛖᚦᛃᛠᛡᚠ ᛋᚦᚢᚹᛝ ᛝᛗᛗᚱᚦᚨᛇᛁ ᚾᛟᚦᛡᛏᚹᛃᛉᛖ ᚨᚱᛟ ᛇᚷᛝᛠᛃᛃᚢᚹᛋᛡᚲᛃ ᛁᚹᛏ ᛚᚨᛃᚹᛃᛖᛡᚨᛉ ᛞᛖ ᚱᛖᛒ ᛉᚺᛡᛇᛇᛁ ᛃᛖᛚᛉᛡᛏᛇᚨᚹᛒᛁᚢ ᛗᛁᛁ ᚹᚹᚾᛡᛡᛇᛖᛃᛠᛞᚾᛞᛉᛈ ᛗᛁᛚᛉᛇᚨ ᛈᛏ ᛈᛗᛚᛉᛃ ᛏᛃᛏᛋᛋᛡᛁᛁ ᚠᛖᚷᛈᚷᚹᛞᚦᛈ ᛞᚦᛠᛚ ᛡᛋᛁᛏᛟᚢ ᛠᛋᚨᛠᚷ ᛉᚦᚺᛚᚷᛡᛡᛟᛚᚲᛒᛉᚠ ᛋᛠ ᚷᚲᚾ ᛒᛏᛟᛟ ᚷᚹ ᚹᚺᚹᛖᚷ ᚷᛠᛁᛞᛏᛇ ᚹᛃᚱ ᚢᛝᚹᚱ ᚺ ᛁᚠᛠᚹ ᛞᚨ ᛏᚱᚠᚺᚦ ᛉᚦᛡᚦ ᚾᛒᛟ ᛞᛁᛁ ᛈᚨᚦᛡ ᛇᚢᚺᛇᚢᛝᚱᛉᛁ ᛟᛇᚱ ᛝᛁᛠ ᛖᛞᚲᚲᚢᛏᛃᚹᚹ ᚲᛝᛗ ᛚᛃᚦᛗᚹᛁᚢᚲ ᛇᛋ ᚨᚦᛁᛞᛒᚠ ᛋᛗᛏᚺᚠᛟ ᛚᛋᚷᛁᛠᚱ ᛉᛁᚲᛡᚷ ᛟᛏᚠᛚᚢᚾ ᛗᛠᛒᛡ ᛝᛡᚦᚱᚹᚾᚷᛚ ᚦᛗᚱᛃᚲᛠᚢ ᛗᛈᛁ ᛋᛠᛗᛟᚠᚨᛡᚢᛈ ᛋ ᛝᚺᚾᛝ ᛉᚺᛖ ᛞᛈᛁᛋᛞᛟ ᚾᚦ ᛗᛖᚲᛟᚠᚹᚾᛉᛃᛒ ᚱᛋᚨᚨᛡᛝᛒᚱᛟᚺ ᛉᛞ ᛝᛗᛁᛗᛉᛈᚺᚹᚲᛚᛟᛡ ᛋᚠᚷᛗ ᚲᛒ ᛖᛉᚷᛞᛋᚨᛖᛗᚲ ᛖᛝᛡᛗᚱ ᛉ ᛁᚦᛝᚱᛉᛇᚱᚹᛈ ᛡᛗᛋᚠᛟᛁᛏᚨᛇᚷᚠ ᚲᛇᛈᛉᚢᚢ ᛇᛞᛠᛖᛝᛡ ᛒᛉ ᛉᛏᛈ ᚾᚨᛞᛒᛡ ᛖᛚᛇ ᛝᚷᛋᚦᛞᛖᛟᚷᚱ ᚱᚾᚦᚦᚾᛝ ᚲᚾᛋᚢᚺ ᚠᛃᛒᛠᛋᚨᛟᚠᚷᛒᛏ ᛏᛖᛏ ᚷᛒᛉᚢᛁᚢᛋ ᚦᚨ ᛃᛁᚢᛟᛗᛈᛏᛏᚷᛋ ᛉᛏᚱᛏᛇᚦ ᛁᛉᚷ ᛉᛖᛗᛈᚨ ᛟᚲᛈᚾᛋ ᛝ ᛚᚱᛡᛟ ᛁᛗᛖᛉᛡᚹᛖ ᛞᛃᛏᛁᛚᛒ ᛉᛉᛁᚢᛉᚺ ᛃᚹᚠᚨ ᚹᚢ ᛒᛗᛡᛠᚾᛃ ᛞᛝᚹᛗᚠᚦ ᛋᛁᛏᚱ ᛟᛋᚹᚱᛋᛒᚢᚨᛁᛈ ᚦ ᛈᛞᛚᛚᚢᚨᛖ ᛃᛞᛞᛁᚲᛉᛠ ᚺᛈᛉ ᚷᚷᛇᚲ ᛇᚺᛡᛞᚹᚾᚢᛇᛞ ᛒᛗᚹᛃ ᛠᚱᚷᛞᛖ ᛝᛠᛡᛖ ᛉᚱᛠᛒᛒᚢᚦ ᛠ ᛏᚹᚱᛈᛈ ᛡᛈᛁᛉᛟᛖ
